REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF STATE BANK OF TEXAS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO ESTABLISH IRREPARABLE HARM OR HARM THAT CANNOT BE COMPENSATED BY MONEY DAMAGES. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-21-2026 Off Calendar

PLAINTIFF STATE BANK OF TEXAS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION
Off Calendar Jan-28-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Josette Johnson, Esq. appearing for Plaintiff State Bank of Texas via Zoom Video Webinar.
Gregory Rougeau, Esq. appearing for Defendant Nishu Jain.

Hearing held.
After hearing arguments, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF STATE BANK OF TEXAS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION

is DENIED. Moving party failed to establish irreparable harm or harm that cannot be compensated by money damages.

Prevailing Party to submit proposed order that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)
